name: Money Management International
description_short: Nonprofit credit counseling and financial education organization providing debt management, bankruptcy counseling, and housing guidance through phone, online, and in-person services.
description_long:
Money Management International (MMI) is a longstanding nonprofit organization dedicated to providing financial counseling and education to consumers struggling with debt, budgeting, housing, and student loan concerns. Founded and operating as a member of the National Foundation for Credit Counseling (NFCC) since 1951, MMI has established itself as a trusted resource in the credit counseling industry with nationwide service availability.

MMI offers a comprehensive suite of financial counseling services including debt management plans, debt resolution plans, bankruptcy counseling, homebuyer education, reverse mortgage counseling, and student loan counseling. Consumers can access these services through multiple channels: 24/7 phone counseling via toll-free line (866-550-8004), self-directed online counseling, and in-person appointments at their Bronx location (903 Sheridan Ave., 2nd floor). The organization also provides extensive free educational resources including blog posts, budget guides, podcasts, webinars, workshops, and specialized military family education.

MMI distinguishes itself through multiple professional certifications and accreditations. The organization holds an A+ rating from the Better Business Bureau, HUD certification for consumer housing counseling, accreditation from the Council on Accreditation (COA), membership in the Financial Counseling Association of America (FCAA), and an "Excellent" rating on Trustpilot. These credentials reflect the organization's commitment to maintaining high service standards and consumer protection.

As a nonprofit credit counseling agency, MMI is genuinely positioned to help consumers at no or low cost, with counselors trained to provide personalized financial guidance. The primary consideration is that while their services are comprehensive, consumers should verify current service availability and pricing structures, as the website does not explicitly state service costs or detail the specific debt management plan fee structures that many nonprofit credit counseling agencies charge.
address: 3636 S Geyer Rd #100, St. Louis, MO 63127

company_info.headquarters: 3636 S Geyer Rd #100, St. Louis, MO 63127

pros: HUD-certified for consumer housing counseling, ensuring compliance with federal standards
24/7 telephone counseling availability via toll-free number removes scheduling barriers
A+ Better Business Bureau rating and "Excellent" Trustpilot rating provide transparent third-party validation
Multiple access channels (phone, online, in-person) accommodate different consumer preferences and circumstances
NFCC member since 1951 with Council on Accreditation certification ensures established credibility
Offers specialized counseling for bankruptcy, homebuying, reverse mortgages, and student loans—not just general debt counseling
Free educational resources (blog, guides, podcasts, webinars) provide supplemental financial literacy
cons: In-person Bronx location has limited hours (Tues-Thurs, 9 a.m.-3 p.m.) and requires appointment scheduling
Website does not disclose specific fees, pricing, or cost structure for debt management plans, which many credit counseling agencies charge
No explicit information about average debt reduction amounts, timeline to debt freedom, or success metrics
Limited geographic presence—only one physical location listed despite nationwide online/phone services
No information about financial hardship policies, fee waivers, or sliding scale options for low-income consumers
best_for: Consumers seeking nonprofit, accredited credit counseling without predatory debt settlement practices
Homebuyers and homeowners needing HUD-certified housing counseling or reverse mortgage guidance
Individuals considering bankruptcy who need certified pre-bankruptcy counseling
Military families accessing specialized financial education resources
diagnosis: MMI is best for consumers seeking genuine nonprofit credit counseling from an accredited, HUD-certified organization with transparent third-party ratings. The main caveat is that specific pricing and fee structures are not disclosed on the website, and consumers should contact MMI directly to understand whether services are truly free or involve management plan fees typical of credit counseling agencies.
services: Debt management plans customized to individual financial situations
HUD-certified homebuyer counseling and education courses
Financial review and custom savings analysis
Free budget guides and educational resources
Webinars and workshops on financial topics
Blog content and podcast education on financial rights and planning
